articles.title: YOUR AI WRITES CODE FASTER THAN YOU CAN REVIEW IT.
articles.summary: Code review is the bottleneck. Reviews are slow, inconsistent, and noisy. AI tools flag everything or miss critical issues.

articles.title: HOW AGENTIC RAG WORKS
articles.summary: Standard RAG is a one-shot pipeline: query in, retrieve, generate. However, it breaks down the moment a question is ambiguous, the answer is spread across multiple sources, or the retrieved chunk looks relevant but isn't. Agentic RAG fixes this by adding an LLM in the middle that can rewrite the query, route to the right source, evaluate whether what came back is actually good enough, and loop again if not.

articles.title: NODE.JS WORKER THREADS ARE PROBLEMATIC, BUT THEY WORK GREAT FOR US
articles.summary: Inngest encountered event loop starvation in its Node.js-based Connect service, where CPU-heavy user code blocked critical heartbeats, causing "no available worker" errors. Their solution was to move the Connect service's internals, like WebSocket connections and heartbeats, into a separate Node.js worker thread to isolate them from user code execution.

articles.title: SO WHERE ARE ALL THE AI APPS?
articles.summary: AI tools have not created any overall increase in new package creation or a substantial general uptick in package updates across the board since the advent of modern AI. However, there has been a significant boost in update frequency.

articles.title: CODE AS A TOOL OF PROCESS
articles.summary: Programming is an iterative process that sharpens one's understanding by confronting intricate details and questions as features are built. However, generating code with AI skips this sharpening process, preventing developers from deeply understanding the complexities and trade-offs involved.

articles.title: TURN YOUR AI AGENTS INTO AUTONOMOUS DEVELOPERS
articles.summary: AI agents generate code fast, but without ever seeing actual API responses, database state, or how your services really behave. mirrord (5k GitHub stars [12]) gives agents real-world context as they code, then lets them test against real staging, see real errors, and iterate until it works. The agent delivers working code, not just a first

articles.title: EMAIL.MD
articles.summary: Email.md converts Markdown into email-safe HTML that renders correctly across all major email clients. Writing email HTML is challenging due to a wide variety of rendering engines, like Outlook's Word engine and Gmail's CSS stripping. Email.md solves this by allowing users to write simple Markdown, which it then transforms into HTML.

articles.title: HYPOTHESIS, ANTITHESIS, SYNTHESIS
articles.summary: Hegel is a new family of property-based testing libraries. It currently supports Rust with plans for Go, C++, OCaml, and TypeScript. It uses the Python Hypothesis engine for its core logic.

articles.title: VIDEO.JS V10 BETA: HELLO, WORLD (AGAIN)
articles.summary: Video.js is an open source media player framework for Web and React. It has been modernized, with reduced bundle sizes by up to 88%. It has also been prepped for AI-augmented development.

articles.title: I WANTED TO BUILD VERTICAL SAAS FOR PEST CONTROL. I TOOK A TECHNICIAN JOB INSTEAD
articles.summary: This dev took a technician job to gain firsthand experience in the pest control industry. This role revealed operational inefficiencies, including slow recruitment, outdated systems, and a lack of incentives for internal innovation. He decided against selling SaaS to existing companies, opting instead to launch his own pest control business.

articles.title: THE DEATH OF MODEL.FIT(): WHAT DATA SCIENTISTS ACTUALLY DO IN THE AGE OF AI AGENTS
articles.summary: In agentic AI systems, data scientists don't train models. Rather, they own the evaluation infrastructure that keeps agent behavior measurable and improvable. That means building error taxonomies from production traces, maintaining golden datasets of real user inputs, calibrating LLM-as-judge against human raters, and gating deploys with deterministic schema checks.

articles.title: IS ANYBODY ELSE BORED OF TALKING ABOUT AI?
articles.summary: This dev finds the pervasive online and workplace discussions solely focused on AI boring, as most.

articles.title: SMALL PROGRAMMING TRICKS
articles.summary: A lot of engineering productivity comes from small, atomic knowledge, and this post shares a list of small programming tricks across various domains.

articles.title: NO, WINDOWS START DOES NOT USE REACT
articles.summary: The Windows Start menu is not built with React, and only its small "Recommended" section uses React Native for Windows.

articles.title: ALGORITHM VISUALIZER
articles.summary: This is an interactive platform to visualize algorithms from code easily.
